entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary crop studied in the lowland cropping systems.

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: Organic material in the soil, crucial for livelihoods and food security.

entities.name: LOW INPUT FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Farming systems with minimal external inputs, typical of smallholders.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where the farming systems are located.

entities.name: RAINFED LOWLAND RICE EXPERIMENT
entities.description: A long-term study testing the effect of plant material additions on rice.

entities.name: SLOWLY DECOMPOSABLE PLANT MATERIALS
entities.description: Plant materials with low quality that decompose slowly.

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: A component of soil organic matter that increased with leaf litter application.

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of rice grain produced.

entities.name: NUTRIENT RECOVERY
entities.description: The efficiency of recovering nutrients like nitrogen and sulfur.

entities.name: LEAF LITTER APPLICATIONS
entities.description: The process of adding leaf litter to the soil over 9 seasons.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A nutrient whose recovery is affected by residue decomposition.

entities.name: SULFUR
entities.description: A nutrient whose recovery is affected by residue decomposition.

entities.name: DECOMPOSITION RATE
entities.description: The speed at which added residues break down.

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to sustain crop growth.
